config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas segalanya, itu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau berbeda pendapat. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan dasar setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically override the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I have seen how strong emphasis on collective goals can bring social stability and rapid development, but I have also seen how easily "for the public good" can become a justification for silencing ordinary people or ignoring personal dignity. A society is not truly healthy if it treats individual rights as disposable whenever the majority or the authorities claim a greater interest. The better balance is to protect basic rights firmly, while allowing limited tradeoffs only when they are necessary, transparent, and genuinely accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya sebagai orang Indonesia, saya melihat dalih “demi kepentingan bersama” sering sekali dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, menormalkan ketidakadilan, atau memaksa orang ikut arus tanpa perlindungan yang jelas. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi justru akan lebih kuat kalau hak dasar setiap orang dijaga dulu, karena tanpa itu “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi alat kekuasaan. Jadi, bagi saya, yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan individu sebagai korban demi kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual, because that logic is too easy to abuse. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value collective stability and shared goals, since they can bring order and development, but I have also seen how the phrase "for the common good" can be used to silence ordinary people or excuse unfair treatment. A healthy society should protect basic rights first, then ask people to make sacrifices only in limited, necessary, and accountable situations. If individual dignity is treated as secondary all the time, then society may become strong on the surface but weak in justice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dalam pengalaman saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan terhadap orang kecil, menutup kritik, atau memaksa kepatuhan tanpa perlindungan yang adil. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas; kalau tidak, kolektif bisa berubah menjadi alat kekuasaan, bukan kebaikan bersama. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir ketika martabat dan kebebasan individu dihormati lebih dulu, lalu kepentingan umum dibangun di atas fondasi itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be treated as automatically higher than the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize collective goals, because social stability and shared development can matter a lot, but I have also seen how easily “for the common good” becomes a reason to ignore ordinary people’s dignity or silence criticism. I do not think a healthy society is one that asks individuals to sacrifice basic rights whenever the collective demands it; that kind of thinking is too easy to abuse. The better approach is to protect core individual rights first, and only accept limited, transparent, accountable tradeoffs in truly necessary situations.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
